DEFENDANT'S STATEMENT THAT THEY ARE "NOT SET UP" FOR SECTION 8 CAN BE REASONABLY CONSTRUED AS AN ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTIVELY DENIED A HOUSING OPPORTUNITY AND/OR HAS A POLICY OF DISCRIMINATION AGAINST SECTION 8 VOUCHER HOLDERS. AS SUCH, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NEED TO ALLEGE DETAILS OF HIS PARTICULAR VOUCHER AT THIS STAGE. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF ALLEGED COMMUNICATIONS WITH DEFENDANT'S AGENT AND THAT DEFENDANT ADVERTISES AND RENTS THE PROPERTY OFF OF ZILLOW. FINALLY, DEFENDANT'S CLAIM TO HAVE A LEGITIMATE, NON-DISCRIMINATORY RATIONALE GOES BEYOND THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION. A FAILURE TO BE AWARE OF RENTAL LAWS DOES NOT CONSTITUTE NEGLIGENCE. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5 IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY THAT A SINGLE INSTANCE OF DISCRIMINATION IN A PRE-CONTRACTUAL SETTING, THROUGH SUCH A BRIEF EXCHANGE OF WORDS, IS EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS. PLAINTIFF'S CITED CASES INVOLVING TENANTS AND LANDLORDS WHO WERE IN PRIVITY, A CASE INVOLVING A THREAT OF RACIAL VIOLENCE, AND HABITABILITY ISSUES. Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One; Requests For Admission, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES-GENERAL, SET ONE; REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE; AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Leonidas De Castro's motion to compel defendant Sanger Vicksburg Street LP (Vicksburg) to provide further responses to form interrogatories general set one, requests for admission set one, and requests for production of documents set one and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the the request for further responses and granted in part as to monetary sanctions. No later than June 6, 2025 Vicksburg must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections to all discovery requests covered by this motion and must pay sanctions in the amount of $2,000 to plaintiff. Vicksburg's request for monetary sanctions is denied.

Vicksburg's hyper-technical, non-substantive, partially boilerplate and partially inaccurate objections to the discovery propounded on it and its refusal to provide any substantive responses is contrary to the self-executing nature of civil discovery and the important role of the meet and confer process to resolve civil discovery disputes. The primary purpose of civil discovery is to eliminate gamesmanship from the resolution of civil disputes, yet Vicksburg's conduct in responding to the discovery propounded on it is properly characterized as gamesmanship.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT CAROL REEN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are OVERRULED. Defendant's statement that they are "not set up" for Section 8 can be reasonably construed as an allegation that Defendant constructively denied a housing opportunity and/or has a policy of discrimination against Section 8 voucher holders. As such, Plaintiff does not need to allege details of his particular voucher at this stage. Further, Plaintiff alleged communications with Defendant's agent and that Defendant advertises and rents the property off of Zillow. Finally, Defendant's claim to have a legitimate, non-discriminatory rationale goes beyond the facts alleged in the Complaint. The demurrer to cause of action 4 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend a viable cause of action. A failure to be aware of rental laws does not constitute negligence. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend a viable cause of action. Plaintiff cites no authority that a single instance of discrimination in a pre-contractual setting, through such a brief exchange of words, is extreme and outrageous. Plaintiff's cited cases involving tenants and landlords who were in privity, a case involving a threat of racial violence, and habitability issues.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
